zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabelle Porter於1333年6月29日出生在英國哈普爾笛卡爾 巴辛時，她的父親Hugh Porter23歲，母親Mirabel Wake26歲。她於1347年8月與Henry de Burghersh在英國素可布瓦申結婚。她去世於1393年10月16日在英國賀特福爾貝可哈姆斯泰德享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabelle Porter于1333年6月29日出生在英国哈普爾笛卡爾 巴辛时，她的父亲Hugh Porter23岁，母亲Mirabel Wake26岁。她于1347年8月与Henry de Burghersh在英国素可布瓦申结婚。她去世于1393年10月16日在英国賀特福爾貝可哈姆斯泰德享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Porter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, castle, or monastery, from Middle English and Older Scots porter(e), port(o)ur ‘doorkeeper, gatekeeper’ (Anglo-Norman French port(i)er, portur, Latin portarius). The office often came with accommodation, lands, and other privileges for the bearer, and in some cases was hereditary, especially in the case of a royal castle. The name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, for example German Pförtner (see Fortner ) and Poertner .

English: occupational name for a man who carried loads for a living, especially one who used his own muscle power rather than a beast of burden or a wheeled vehicle. This sense is from Middle English port(o)ur, porter ‘porter, carrier of burdens’ (Anglo-Norman French portur, porteo(u)r).
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized, of Poorter, status name for a freeman (burgher) of a town, Middle Dutch portere, modern Dutch poorter. Compare De Porter .

variants.name: Porterfield
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English porter(e) ‘gatekeeper, doorkeeper’ + feld ‘open country, bounded piece of land’, referring to the hereditary lands owned by a porter of a monastery (see Porter 1), taken as a surname by the descendants of such an official. Black suggests that they would originally have been known as Porter but extended their name when ‘territorial surnames’ became fashionable.

variants.name: Sluiter
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a porter or gatekeeper, Middle Dutch sluter. Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Port
meanings: English: from Middle English port(e) ‘gateway, entrance’ (Old French porte, from Latin porta), hence a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town or city, or for the gatekeeper. Compare Porter 1.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a harbor or in a market town, from a homonymous Middle English port(e) (Old English port ‘harbor, market town’, from Latin portus ‘harbor, haven’, reinforced in Middle English by Old French port, from the same source).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Port‐en‐Bessin (Calvados), which is recorded as Portu in 1035–36. The placename derives from Latin portus ‘port, harbour, haven’.

variants.name: Fortner
meanings: German: variant (also Förtner) or Americanized form of Pförtner, an occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper in a great house, castle, or monastery, Middle High German phortenaere (from Latin porta ‘door, gate’). Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Portmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Helmut, Franz, Hansi, Kurt.

North German: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Middle Low German port(e) ‘gate’ (from Latin porta) + man ‘man’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town. Compare Porter .

variants.name: Portier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Clovis, Leonce, Renauld.
French: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, Old French portier (from Late Latin portarius). Compare English Porter .
History: Alexandre-Bernard Portier from Paris, France, married Catherine Monte in Edgard, LA, in 1741.

nameMeanings.name: Isabelle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabelle Porter was born on 29 June 1333, in Old Basing, Hampshire, England, her father, Hugh Porter, was 23 and her mother, Mirabel Wake, was 26. She married Henry de Burghersh in August 1347, in Burwash, Sussex, England. She died on 16 October 1393, in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabelle Porter est née le 29 juin 1333, à Old Basing, Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Hugh Porter, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Mirabel Wake, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Henry de Burghersh en août 1347, à Burwash, Sussex, Angleterre. Elle est décédée le 16 octobre 1393, à Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabelle Porter am 29. Juni 1333 in Old Basing, Hampshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Porter 23 und ihre Mutter Mirabel Wake 26. Sie hat im August 1347 in Burwash, Sussex, England Henry de Burghersh geheiratet. Sie starb am 16. Oktober 1393 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabelle Porter è nata il il 29 giugno 1333 nel Old Basing, Hampshire, England, sua padre, Hugh Porter, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Mirabel Wake, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Henry de Burghersh nel agosto 1347, nel Burwash, Sussex, England. Morì il 16 ottobre 1393, nel Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, England, all'età di 60 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabelle Porter(이)가 1333년 6월 29일 영국 함프실르 드 바징에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Porter(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mirabel Wake(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1347년 8월 영국 수세스 버와시에서 Henry de Burghersh(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1393년 10월 16일 영국 허트퍼드실르 버카스테드에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.29 June 1333–16 October 1393: 29 de junho de 1333–16 de outubro de 1393
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabelle Porter nasceu em 29 de junho de 1333, em Old Basing, Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh Porter, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Mirabel Wake, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry de Burghersh em agosto de 1347, em Burwash, Sussex, Inglaterra. Ela faleceu em 16 de outubro de 1393, em Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabelle Porter родилась 29 июня 1333 г. в местности олд басинг, хампшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Hugh Porter было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Mirabel Wake, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry de Burghersh август 1347 г. в местности бурваш, суссекс, Англия. Она умерла 16 октября 1393 г. в местности бирхамстед, хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 60 лет.

es.29 June 1333–16 October 1393: 29 de junio de 1333–16 de octubre de 1393
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabelle Porter nació el 29 de junio de 1333, en Old Basing, Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Hugh Porter, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Mirabel Wake, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Henry de Burghersh en agosto de 1347, en Burwash, Sussex, Inglaterra. Murió el 16 de octubre de 1393, en Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 60 años.
